stories.title: Starmer says forced adoptions a 'stain on our history' — issues formal state apology
stories.summary: Sir Keir Starmer issued a formal government apology on behalf of the British state for historical forced adoptions in England and Wales between 1949 and 1976, affecting an estimated 185,000 babies taken from unmarried mothers.

stories.title: Labour failed to prepare for power, admits PM's former top aide Morgan McSweeney
stories.summary: In his first-ever media interview, Morgan McSweeney — Starmer's former chief of staff — admitted Labour was not prepared to govern quickly enough when it took office in 2024, saying he needed to 'move on to a new chapter' in his life.

stories.title: Keir Starmer's tenure at No. 10 drawing to a close
stories.summary: Analysis from The Guardian suggests PM Starmer is in his final days at Downing Street, with the Labour Party facing a leadership contest as his authority wanes following poor local election results.

topics.name: Parliament
topics.description: House of Commons, House of Lords, PMQs & Legislation

stories.title: Immigration and Asylum Bill 2026 introduced in House of Commons
stories.summary: The Immigration and Asylum Bill 2026 was introduced in the House of Commons on 30 June 2026, with its second reading scheduled for 13 July. It implements changes to immigration and asylum policy announced by the government.

stories.title: Commons Business: Debate on ban on trade with illegal Israeli settlements scheduled
stories.summary: This week's House of Commons business includes a general debate on 9 July on a ban on trade with illegal Israeli settlements, alongside a debate on the impact of strategic lawsuits against public participation.

stories.title: Commonhold and Leasehold Reform — Managing Agents debate in Commons
stories.summary: The House of Commons held a debate on Commonhold and Leasehold Reform focusing on managing agents, providing members the opportunity to review the draft Bill and identify measures needed in the final version.

stories.title: Labour was not prepared to govern in 2024, says Morgan McSweeney
stories.summary: Starmer's former chief of staff gave his first public interview admitting political mistakes and revealed that Trump shared theories about wind turbines with him. McSweeney acknowledged Labour failed to deliver quickly enough after winning the 2024 election.

stories.title: Andy Burnham emerges as frontrunner in Labour leadership race
stories.summary: Andy Burnham, the Manchester Mayor, has become the frontrunner to succeed Keir Starmer as Labour leader and Prime Minister. Former Labour MP Joan Walley praised Burnham's time in Manchester as providing a different perspective for Westminster.

stories.title: 'The shame is ours': Starmer issues formal state apology over forced adoptions
stories.summary: PM Keir Starmer apologised for the state's role in the forced adoption of an estimated 185,000 babies from unmarried mothers between 1949 and 1976. The Guardian's live politics blog covered the announcement extensively.

stories.title: Kemi Badenoch seeks advice from former Tory leaders to boost PMQs performance
stories.summary: Conservative leader Kemi Badenoch has spoken to all her living predecessors — except one — as she seeks to sharpen her performance at Prime Minister's Questions. Boris Johnson reportedly cautioned that Nigel Farage comes with the 'whiff of sulphur'.

stories.title: Nigel Farage reported to parliamentary standards watchdog over alleged 'crypto lobbying'
stories.summary: The parliamentary standards commissioner has been asked to investigate Reform UK leader Nigel Farage after a private meeting he held with the Bank of England governor raised concerns about undisclosed crypto lobbying.

stories.title: Bitcoin firm advertised by Nigel Farage loses 15% of asset value
stories.summary: A bitcoin treasury company promoted by Farage has seen its assets plunge by 15%, with finance experts warning against investing in such companies. The news adds to scrutiny of Farage's paid promotional activities.

stories.title: Farage paid £270,000 for 12 hours' work promoting gold bullion company
stories.summary: An investigation reveals Nigel Farage was paid £270,000 for just 12 hours of work promoting Direct Bullion, raising questions about financial conflicts of interest for the Reform UK leader.

stories.title: Kate Forbes: I was 'slam dunk' for SNP leadership until revealing views on same-sex marriage
stories.summary: Former SNP Deputy First Minister Kate Forbes has said she was the frontrunner for the SNP leadership until she publicly revealed her views on same-sex marriage, and claims she was advised to lie about her opinions.

stories.title: Is Andy Burnham simply Labour's Boris Johnson? SNP seizes on devolution comments
stories.summary: The Herald Scotland analyses whether Andy Burnham's comments on devolution have handed the SNP an open goal, with the presumptive PM seen as making the case for Scottish independence through his centralising instincts.

stories.title: Get Britain Working: Update on the Jobs and Careers Service
stories.summary: The government published an update on the Jobs and Careers Service, outlining how local labour market plans will integrate partners to support employment and earnings, as part of the broader 'Get Britain Working' agenda.

stories.title: Environment Secretary Emma Reynolds addresses Groundswell Festival 2026
stories.summary: Environment Secretary Emma Reynolds delivered a speech at the Groundswell Festival 2026, setting out the government's approach to sustainable farming and environmental policy.

topics.name: Elections & By-elections
topics.description: Upcoming or recent election results & polls

stories.title: Labour leadership contest triggered — MP issues ultimatum to cabinet members
stories.summary: Former Labour minister Catherine West told cabinet members to challenge Keir Starmer for the leadership by Monday or she would do so herself, claiming 10 MPs were ready to back her and triggering a formal contest.

stories.title: Local elections handed Reform gains — and triggered Starmer's departure
stories.summary: Recent local elections saw significant Reform UK gains that shattered the assumption Reform posed the ultimate threat to Labour, ultimately contributing to the political pressure that has led Starmer to announce he will leave office.

stories.title: House of Lords: Motions for Balloted Debate — 3 July 2026
stories.summary: The House of Lords published its Order Paper for 3 July 2026, with motions for balloted debates covering a range of policy areas.

stories.title: House of Lords Justice & Home Affairs Committee publishes major immigration report
stories.summary: The Lords Justice & Home Affairs Committee released a 122-page report scrutinising the government's proposed Immigration and Asylum Bill, which was introduced in the Commons on 30 June 2026.
